author: @agentbertram4769
text:
Re water from an engineers view. Great stuff is water. When it's ice you can put heat into it. When it's water you can put heat into it. When it's hot water you can put heat into it. When it's steam you can put heat into it. When it's HP steam you can put heat into it. When it's superheated steam you can put heat into it. Without water we'd be sunk... :O)
Good balance Rob. A most enjoyable video.

author: @thethoughtemporium
text: I always went with the 2 scale method, where to get the full range of weights you get one scale that is much mroe corse and one that is very fine. Since most often if you're using lots of stuff, you don't need it to be precise and if you're using a tiny bit of stuff, you're still covered. Still a great video though

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +The Thought Emporium cheers mate

author: @anonymoususer6334
text:
Hi Robert, I enjoy your videos very much. While there are several reasons, not the least of which is good information and ideas. But on a purely entertainment level i'd have to say that your enthusiasm is always high. Whether re-purposing a $10 shelf or discovering a potential windfall technology, you seem to always work hard and enjoy hard word. Hard used to include long hours, on your feet, multi tasking projects, trial and error repetitive chores as well as physically and/or mentally demanding tasks.
So, since your balance will either be in equilibrium or not, and your need is to be. Put stops at either end of the fulcrum or better yet lower the pivot. Any amount off center indicates adjustment needed so a 1/16th inch off center at the indicator above fulcrum is plenty 1/8th inch total variance, but the small relative difference in movement at platforms leaves both hands to tune and removes much of the interference caused physical tremors. Easing considerably the task intended.
Also, if you made the balance platforms out of sturdy wire like coat hanger, with the eye above center, and 3 legs down attached to a ring, large enough to hold your small containers you reduce error and speed setup and could make an alt. platform for the product side to weigh bulkier but light materials.
eliminating all the weight at the platforms with allow for easier balancing and not hurt tolerances much. you could then also crimp your weights for a rough balance point and at each end of the fulcrum arm where the baskets hang insert a 1.5" very fine thread #8 or smaller screw approx 1/4" into the end with 1 nut on each that moves in/out as needed and there is your quick fine tune and sensitivity adjustments.
You can use water, small lead shot, coins n currency or comparative items as your goal. check and adjust as needed to maintain balance point. i hope this description is clear enough to follow, i sometimes think so to be told later i wrote gibberish.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Anonymous User it's more than clear mate and thanks very much for the input - other potential builders should read it

replies.author: @anonymoususer6334
replies.text: Cheers Mate, to success!
